winner.title: End-to-end scope monotonicity proof across delegation chain
winner.description: The Delegation circuit enforces delegateeScope & ~delegatorScope == 0 per hop, but there is no formal proof that composing N hops preserves monotonicity end-to-end (i.e., that the final delegatee's scope is a subset of the original delegator's scope). Write a formal inductive proof: base case (hop 0 scope ⊆ AgentPolicy scope via scopeCommitment binding), inductive step (if hop k scope ⊆ hop k-1 scope, then hop k+1 scope ⊆ hop k scope). Encode as machine-checkable properties using either Lean 4 or as Circom witness-generation test vectors covering 1-, 2-, and 3-hop chains with attempted scope escalation at each hop.

outline.title: End-to-end scope monotonicity proof across delegation chain

artifacts.description: Formal inductive proof document: base case (hop-0 scope ⊆ AgentPolicy scopeCommitment), inductive step (hop k+1 scope ⊆ hop k scope via bitwise AND-mask), and corollary that N-hop composition preserves subset relationship end-to-end.

Mocha witness-generation tests covering 1-, 2-, and 3-hop delegation chains. Each chain tests: (a) valid narrowing passes, (b) lateral scope expansion (same bits) fails, (c) scope escalation (new bit set) fails at each hop position, (d) escalation on final hop fails even when intermediate hops are valid.

Optional helper template DelegationChainN(N) that composes N Delegation instances sequentially and exposes a single public output asserting finalScope is a bitwise subset of rootScope. Used as a gadget to make the chain property circuit-native rather than relying solely on off-circuit composition.

Append section 'Scope Monotonicity — Chain Induction' to existing formal properties doc. Enumerates the three circuit constraints that enforce the invariant: (1) scopeCommitment binding in AgentPolicy, (2) delegateeScope & ~delegatorScope == 0 in Delegation, (3) chain composition via DelegationChainN.

Machine-readable test-vector file: each entry is {hops, rootScope, intermediateScopes[], finalScope, expectedValid}. Covers 24 adversarial cases: escalation at hop 0, hop 1, hop 2, final hop, and multi-hop simultaneous escalation attempts.

steps: Read Delegation.circom and AgentPolicy.circom in full to map existing constraint names (scopeCommitment, delegatorScope, delegateeScope) and signal wire identifiers.

Formalize base case in spec: show that AgentPolicy's scopeCommitment Poseidon hash binds the agent's scope to its credential; any witness with a scope not matching the commitment fails the circuit's hash-equality constraint.

Formalize inductive step in spec: for any hop k, the constraint `delegateeScope & ~delegatorScope == 0` is equivalent to `delegateeScope ⊆ delegatorScope` in the 8-bit cumulative encoding. Prove this holds for all 256 possible 8-bit values.

Prove chain corollary by induction: if S_0 is the root scope, S_1 ⊆ S_0, S_2 ⊆ S_1, …, then S_N ⊆ S_0 by transitivity of subset. Document in spec/scope-monotonicity-proof.md with explicit bit-level notation.

Design the 24 adversarial test-vector cases covering: (a) single-hop escalation (all 8 bit positions), (b) 2-hop escalation at hop 1, (c) 2-hop escalation at hop 2, (d) 3-hop escalation at each hop, (e) multi-hop simultaneous escalation. Write these to circuits/test/scope_escalation_vectors.json.
Implement scope_monotonicity_chain.test.js: load test vectors, for each vector call snarkjs.wtns.calculate on Delegation circuit, assert witness generation succeeds iff expectedValid === true.

Add DelegationChainN(N) template to circuits/src/DelegationChain.circom: instantiate N Delegation components, wire output delegateeScope of hop k to input delegatorScope of hop k+1, expose rootScope (hop 0 delegatorScope) and finalScope (hop N-1 delegateeScope) as public outputs.
Add a chain-level constraint in DelegationChainN: assert finalScope & ~rootScope == 0 as a redundant top-level check (defense in depth beyond per-hop constraints).
Extend circuits/test/scope_monotonicity_chain.test.js to also exercise DelegationChainN for N=1,2,3 against the same adversarial vectors.
Update circuits/FORMAL-PROPERTIES.md with the new 'Scope Monotonicity — Chain Induction' section, referencing the proof document and linking to the test vectors.
Run npm run test:circuits:fast to confirm all new tests pass and no existing tests regress.
Optionally encode base case and inductive step as Lean 4 lemmas in spec/lean/ScopeMonotonicity.lean for machine-checkable verification outside the circuit toolchain.
dependencies: circuits/src/Delegation.circom
circuits/src/AgentPolicy.circom
circuits/FORMAL-PROPERTIES.md
circuits/build/Delegation.r1cs
circuits/build/AgentPolicy.r1cs

winner.title: Publish deterministic test vectors for all three circuits
winner.description: Create a spec/test-vectors/ directory with JSON files containing known-good inputs, expected public signals, and expected verification results for each circuit (HumanUniqueness, AgentPolicy, Delegation). Include negative cases: expired credential, stale root, scope superset violation, cumulative bit encoding violation. Pin the BN254 curve parameters and Poseidon round constants explicitly. Any independent implementation must be able to reproduce identical outputs from these vectors to claim conformance.

outline.title: Publish deterministic test vectors for all three circuits

artifacts.description: Conformance test vector specification: schema, how to validate, BN254 curve parameters, Poseidon MDS matrix and round constants (t=3, 8 full + 57 partial rounds)
Pinned BN254 field modulus p, scalar field order r, generator G1/G2 affine coordinates
Poseidon round constants and MDS matrix for t=3 and t=5, matching circomlib's implementation exactly
HumanUniqueness circuit: positive case (valid Merkle path + nonce), negative cases: stale root (root not in registry), nullifier reuse (same identity group + nonce), wrong nonce binding

AgentPolicy circuit: positive case (valid EdDSA sig + non-expired + valid cumulative bits), negative cases: expired credential (expiry < currentTime), cumulative bit encoding violation (bit 3 set without bit 2), permissions superset of operator grant
Delegation circuit: positive case (scope subset), negative cases: scope superset violation (delegated bits exceed delegator bits), cumulative bit implication violation in delegated credential

JSON Schema for all vector files: required fields (circuit, version, curve, vectors[]), vector entry schema (id, description, inputs, expected_public_signals, expected_witness_hash, expected_verification_result, tags)

Node.js conformance runner: loads all vector JSON files, runs snarkjs witness generation + verification against pinned .zkey artifacts, asserts public signals and verification result match, prints pass/fail per vector
Mocha test suite that calls validate.js as a subprocess and fails CI if any vector fails; tagged so it runs in both fast (witness-only) and slow (full proof) modes
How an independent implementer reproduces the vectors: install deps, run validate.js, expected output format, how to report discrepancies

steps: Pin BN254 curve parameters (field modulus p, scalar field order r, G1/G2 generators) from the snarkjs/ffjavascript source and write spec/test-vectors/bn254-params.json
Extract Poseidon round constants and MDS matrix for t=3 and t=5 from circomlib (poseidon_constants.js) and write spec/test-vectors/poseidon-constants.json with a deterministic derivation note

Define the JSON Schema for vector files (spec/test-vectors/schema.json): circuit name, curve, snarkjs version, zkey artifact hash, and per-vector fields (inputs, expected_public_signals, expected_witness_hash, expected_verification_result, tags)

Generate HumanUniqueness positive vector: choose fixed secret/identity commitment, build a depth-20 Merkle path with known leaf values, compute nullifierHash and nonceBinding manually via Poseidon, record all inputs and expected public signals

Generate HumanUniqueness negative vectors: (a) stale root — swap Merkle root to a value not matching the path, (b) nullifier reuse — duplicate sessionNonce, (c) wrong nonce binding — mismatched externalNullifier

Generate AgentPolicy positive vector: create deterministic EdDSA keypair (Baby Jubjub), sign modelHash+permissions+expiry, set currentTime < expiry, set valid cumulative bits (e.g. 0b00001111 with all implications satisfied)

Generate AgentPolicy negative vectors: (a) expired credential — set currentTime > expiry, (b) cumulative bit violation — set bit 3 without bit 2, (c) permissions superset — delegated bits exceed operatorGrant
Generate Delegation positive vector: delegatorBits = 0b00001111, delegatedBits = 0b00000011 (strict subset), valid delegator EdDSA sig
Generate Delegation negative vectors: (a) scope superset — delegatedBits has a bit not in delegatorBits, (b) cumulative bit implication violation in delegated credential output
For each vector, run snarkjs witness generation against the compiled .wasm, compute witness hash (SHA-256 of witness array serialized as little-endian uint64 JSON), and record in the vector file
For slow/full-proof vectors, run groth16.prove with the pinned .zkey, record the proof and run groth16.verify, store expected_verification_result

Write spec/test-vectors/validate.js: iterates all JSON files in the directory, resolves .wasm/.zkey paths relative to circuits/build/, asserts public signals match element-by-element, asserts verification result matches, exits nonzero on any failure

Write circuits/test/conformance.test.js Mocha suite that shells out to validate.js and propagates exit code as test pass/fail; fast mode skips full-proof assertion, slow mode (FULL_PROOF=1) asserts verification result
Add conformance step to CI: fast conformance runs on every PR, slow conformance gates releases (label-triggered)

Write spec/test-vectors/README.md documenting curve choice, Poseidon parameterization, vector schema, and how to add new vectors; write CONFORMANCE.md with step-by-step reproduction instructions for external implementers
dependencies: circuits/build/HumanUniqueness.wasm + .zkey must exist (npm run compile:circuits)
circuits/build/AgentPolicy_groth16.zkey + AgentPolicy_plonk.zkey
circuits/build/Delegation_groth16.zkey + Delegation_plonk.zkey
snarkjs >=0.7.6 (pinned in root package.json overrides)
circomlib poseidon_constants.js for constant extraction
ffjavascript for Baby Jubjub EdDSA key generation in vector scripts
pot16.ptau (universal SRS, already in circuits/build/)

winner.title: Off-chain session token for multi-step agent chains
winner.description: LangChain/CrewAI agent chains make 10-50 tool calls per task. Requiring an on-chain handshake per call is prohibitively slow and expensive. Add an SDK-level session token: one on-chain verifyHandshake() mints a signed session token (JWT with proof digest + expiry + scope), and subsequent tool calls verify the token off-chain via the SDK's verifySessionToken(). The on-chain contract gets a batchCheckpoint(bytes32 sessionRoot) for periodic anchoring. This is the single biggest adoption blocker for framework users.

outline.title: Off-chain session token for multi-step agent chains

artifacts.description: Core session token module: SessionToken type, mintSessionToken() called after verifyHandshake(), verifySessionToken() for off-chain per-call verification, revokeSessionToken() for explicit teardown.
Re-export mintSessionToken, verifySessionToken, revokeSessionToken, SessionToken, SessionTokenOptions from public API surface.
Minimal Solidity contract exposing batchCheckpoint(bytes32 sessionRoot, uint64 epoch) and CheckpointRecorded event. Stores sessionRoot → block.timestamp mapping. No ZK verification — anchoring only.

Protocol spec: JWT payload schema (proofDigest, humanNullifier, agentCredHash, scopeBitmap, sessionNonce, iat, exp, iss), signing key lifecycle, off-chain verification algorithm, replay prevention via nonce set, checkpoint anchoring cadence, revocation surface.

Unit tests: mint produces valid JWT; verifySessionToken accepts valid token; rejects expired, tampered, wrong-scope, replayed tokens; scope narrowing at mint time enforces cumulative-bit rules; revokeSessionToken causes subsequent verify to fail.
Hardhat tests: batchCheckpoint emits CheckpointRecorded with correct sessionRoot and epoch; duplicate checkpoint for same epoch reverts; sessionRoot retrieval matches stored value.

Integration guide for LangChain/CrewAI users: initialize session after first handshake, pass SessionToken through tool-call context, configure checkpoint interval, handle expiry mid-chain, migration path from per-call on-chain verify.

steps: Define SessionToken payload schema in spec/session-token-offchain.md: JWT with fields proofDigest (SHA-256 of humanProof+agentProof bytes), humanNullifier, agentCredHash, scopeBitmap (8-bit), sessionNonce (32-byte random), iat, exp, iss='bolyra.ai'. Signing key is an ephemeral ECDSA P-256 key generated per SDK instance, never leaves process memory.

Implement mintSessionToken(humanProof, agentProof, verifiedHandshake, options) in sdk/src/session.ts. Accepts the output of a successful verifyHandshake() call — does not re-verify on-chain, only hashes the proof pair into proofDigest. Options: expirySeconds (default 3600), scopeOverride (must be subset of verifiedHandshake.scopeBitmap, enforced via validateCumulativeBitEncoding()). Returns signed JWT string.

Implement verifySessionToken(token, requiredScope?) in sdk/src/session.ts. Verifies JWT signature with in-process public key, checks exp, checks requiredScope bits are a subset of token.scopeBitmap, checks sessionNonce against in-process revocation set. Returns decoded SessionToken payload or throws typed BolyraSessionError.

Add in-process revocation set (Set<string> of sessionNonce values) to SDK session module. revokeSessionToken(token) decodes without scope check, adds nonce to set. Document that revocation is process-local — distributed revocation requires application-layer propagation or short expiry.

Implement batchCheckpoint() helper in sdk/src/session.ts: accepts array of active SessionToken objects, computes sessionRoot as keccak256 of sorted(sessionNonce[]) merkle leaf set, calls BolyraSessionAnchor.batchCheckpoint(sessionRoot, currentEpoch) on-chain. Intended to be called periodically (e.g., every N tool calls or every M seconds), not per-call.

Write BolyraSessionAnchor.sol: mapping(bytes32 => uint64) public checkpoints; function batchCheckpoint(bytes32 sessionRoot, uint64 epoch) external emits CheckpointRecorded(sessionRoot, epoch, block.timestamp); reverts if checkpoints[sessionRoot] != 0 for same epoch to prevent overwrite.

Write unit tests in sdk/test/session.test.ts covering: happy path mint+verify, expiry rejection, scope narrowing enforcement, scope elevation rejection, replay via nonce reuse, revocation, and tampered signature rejection.
Write Hardhat tests in contracts/test/BolyraSessionAnchor.test.ts covering: checkpoint emission, epoch deduplication revert, and retrieval.

Update sdk/src/index.ts to export new session API. Update sdk/QUICKSTART.md with a 'Session Tokens' section showing the three-line integration pattern: verifyHandshake → mintSessionToken → verifySessionToken in tool handler.

Write docs/session-token-guide.md with LangChain and CrewAI code examples, checkpoint interval recommendations (every 10 calls or 60s, whichever first), expiry handling pattern (re-handshake and re-mint), and security notes on process-local key and revocation scope.
Run npm run test:contracts and cd sdk && npm run build && npm test to verify all new tests pass. DCO sign-off on all commits.
dependencies: verifyHandshake() — session token is only valid downstream of a successful on-chain handshake verification; mintSessionToken() must receive its verified output struct
validateCumulativeBitEncoding() from sdk/src/permissions.ts — enforces 8-bit implication rules on scopeOverride at mint time
BolyraHandshakeVerifier.sol (existing) — batchCheckpoint is a separate contract but references the same sessionNonce domain; deploy order must place anchor after verifier
pot16.ptau and .zkey artifacts — not directly needed for session layer but session proofDigest hashes the proof bytes produced by existing circuit proving pipeline
Node crypto module (built-in) — ECDSA P-256 for JWT signing; no new npm dependencies required
jose or jsonwebtoken npm package — evaluate for JWT signing; prefer jose (ESM-native, no CVEs in current audit) over jsonwebtoken

winner.title: Bind chain ID into sessionNonce to prevent cross-chain proof replay
winner.description: The current sessionNonce is chain-agnostic: a valid handshake proof generated for Base can be replayed on Arbitrum or Polygon if both chains share the same Merkle roots. Fix this by defining sessionNonce = Poseidon2(verifierNonce, chainId) and requiring the contract to enforce block.chainid matches the chainId embedded in the nonce. This is a single-line change in HumanUniqueness and AgentPolicy circuits (add a chainId public input, bind it into the nonce hash), plus a require(chainId == block.chainid) in IdentityRegistry.verifyHandshake().

outline.title: Bind chain ID into sessionNonce to prevent cross-chain proof replay

artifacts.description: Add public input `chainId`, change sessionNonce computation from Poseidon(verifierNonce) to Poseidon2(verifierNonce, chainId). Output chainId as a public signal so the verifier contract can check it.
Same change as HumanUniqueness: add public input `chainId`, rebind sessionNonce = Poseidon2(verifierNonce, chainId), expose chainId as public output.
Propagate chainId public input through delegation proof so delegated credentials also carry chain binding. Delegation sessionNonce must commit to the same chainId as the parent credential.
In verifyHandshake(): extract chainId from proof public signals, add require(chainId == block.chainid, 'chain mismatch'). Update ABI accordingly.
Regenerate Solidity Groth16 verifier from updated .vkey.json after re-running trusted setup with new circuit (extra public input shifts signal indices).
Regenerate both Groth16 and PLONK verifiers from updated .vkey.json files for AgentPolicy circuit.
Regenerate Groth16 and PLONK verifiers for Delegation circuit.

Accept optional `chainId` parameter in createHumanIdentity() and proveHandshake(). Default to current chain via provider.getNetwork().chainId. Compute sessionNonce = poseidon2([verifierNonce, chainId]) before passing inputs to snarkjs.
Accept `chainId` in createAgentCredential() and proveHandshake(). Include chainId in proof inputs. Update ProofInputs type definition.
Add chainId: bigint field to HandshakeProofInputs, ProofResult, and VerifyHandshakeOptions interfaces.
Add chain_id: int field to HandshakeProofInputs and VerifyHandshakeOptions dataclasses. Pass through to subprocess bridge.
Add fast witness tests: valid chainId matches, mismatched chainId produces different nonce, cross-chain replay scenario where same verifierNonce + different chainId yields different sessionNonce.
Parallel tests to humanUniqueness: chainId binding in sessionNonce for both Groth16 and PLONK paths.

Integration tests: (1) valid proof with matching block.chainid succeeds, (2) proof generated for chainId=8453 submitted to a mock chain with chainId=42161 reverts with 'chain mismatch', (3) delegated proof with wrong chainId also reverts.

Update sessionNonce definition in Section 4.2 to sessionNonce = Poseidon2(verifierNonce, chainId). Add new Section 4.3 'Chain Binding' describing why chainId is a mandatory public input and how verifiers enforce it.
Update proof resolution algorithm to note that proof verification is chain-scoped; resolvers MUST check chainId matches the target chain's network identifier.
Update proveHandshake() example to show chainId parameter. Add a note explaining cross-chain replay protection and that proofs are chain-scoped by default.

steps: Update HumanUniqueness.circom: add `signal input chainId` (public), change sessionNonce computation to component nonceHasher = Poseidon(2); nonceHasher.inputs[0] <== verifierNonce; nonceHasher.inputs[1] <== chainId; sessionNonce <== nonceHasher.out; add `signal output chainIdOut <== chainId`
Apply identical chainId public input + nonce rebinding to AgentPolicy.circom and Delegation.circom
Recompile all three circuits: npm run compile:circuits — confirms constraint counts and regenerates .r1cs files

Re-run project-specific Groth16 trusted setup for AgentPolicy and Delegation using existing pot16.ptau (HumanUniqueness reuses Semaphore ceremony — verify depth-20 ceremony still covers new constraint count before proceeding)
Run snarkjs plonk setup for AgentPolicy and Delegation PLONK variants with updated .r1cs
Regenerate all Solidity verifier contracts from new .vkey.json files via snarkjs generateverifier; copy into contracts/src/verifiers/

Update IdentityRegistry.sol: in verifyHandshake(), read chainId from the humanProof and agentProof public signals arrays (new index after existing outputs), add require(humanChainId == block.chainid && agentChainId == block.chainid, 'BolyraRegistry: chain mismatch')
Update sdk/src/types.ts: add chainId field to all affected interfaces
Update sdk/src/humanIdentity.ts and agentCredential.ts: accept chainId, default to provider.getNetwork().chainId, compute nonce with poseidon2([verifierNonce, chainId])
Update sdk-python types and subprocess bridge to pass chain_id through
Write circuit fast tests covering: same verifierNonce + different chainId → different sessionNonce; matching chainId → witness generation succeeds
Write Hardhat integration tests: cross-chain replay reverts, same-chain succeeds, delegated proof cross-chain reverts
Regenerate spec/conformance-test-vectors.json with new chainId field in all proof fixtures
Update spec/draft-bolyra-mutual-zkp-auth-01.md Section 4.2–4.3 and did-method-bolyra.md resolution algorithm
Run full test suite: npm run test:circuits:fast && npm run test:contracts
Run slow proof tests to confirm end-to-end witness + proof + on-chain verify: npm run test:circuits:slow
Bump SDK minor version (breaking change to proof inputs API), update CHANGELOG
dependencies: pot16.ptau — must confirm 2^16 capacity still covers HumanUniqueness with the additional Poseidon2 constraint; new constraint count is baseline + ~2
Semaphore v4 ceremony reuse — HumanUniqueness reuses public ceremony; the extra chainId input adds ~2 constraints; verify ceremony capacity before regenerating zkey
snarkjs >=0.7.6 — already pinned in repo; Poseidon2 component available in circomlib
circomlib Poseidon component — already a dependency; Poseidon(2) is a 2-input variant, confirm import path in existing circuits matches
Hardhat network config — test suite needs a mock chain with a non-default chainId to exercise the mismatch revert path; add a named network entry in hardhat.config.js
Existing .zkey artifacts in circuits/build/ — will be replaced; CI artifact cache must be invalidated after this change
IdentityRegistry ABI — any external consumers of the ABI (landing demo, integration tests, MCP proxy) must be updated to pass chainId in proof public signals

winner.title: Formalize did:bolyra resolution algorithm with DID Core conformance
winner.description: spec/did-method-bolyra.md exists but lacks the required resolution algorithm per W3C DID Core §7.1. Define the concrete steps: parse did:bolyra:<commitment>, query IdentityRegistry for enrollment status and current Merkle root, construct the DID Document with verificationMethod (BabyJubJub key for humans, EdDSA operator key for agents), and authentication/assertionMethod relationships. Include the CRUD operations matrix (Create=enroll, Read=resolve, Update=N/A for humans, Deactivate=revoke). Without this, no universal resolver can implement did:bolyra.

outline.title: Formalize did:bolyra resolution algorithm with DID Core conformance

artifacts.description: Extend existing spec with W3C DID Core §7.1 resolution algorithm: parse did:bolyra:<commitment>, registry lookup, DID Document construction, CRUD matrix, error codes (invalidDid, notFound, deactivated)

Standalone normative resolution algorithm document: step-by-step pseudocode, input/output types (DIDResolutionResult, DIDDocument, DIDResolutionMetadata, DIDDocumentMetadata), content-type negotiation
Add getEnrollmentStatus(commitment) and getMerkleRoot() view functions needed by the resolver; ensure events (Enrolled, Revoked) are indexable for off-chain resolvers

Implement DIDResolver interface: parse DID, call IdentityRegistry, build DIDDocument with verificationMethod (BabyJubJub for humans, EdDSA operator key for agents), authentication/assertionMethod relationships
DID parsing utilities: validate did:bolyra:<commitment> syntax, extract method-specific identifier, detect human vs agent subtype from commitment prefix/format

Conformance tests against DID Core test suite assertions: resolve valid human DID, resolve valid agent DID, return notFound for unknown commitment, return deactivated for revoked identity, verify DIDDocument shape
Machine-readable test vectors: input DIDs, expected DIDDocument fragments, expected resolution metadata error codes for invalid/deactivated/unknown cases
Universal resolver integration guide: how to register did:bolyra driver, example curl against universal-resolver, SDK one-liner resolve() usage

steps: Audit spec/did-method-bolyra.md against W3C DID Core §7.1 checklist — identify all missing normative sections (resolution algorithm, CRUD matrix, error codes, DIDDocumentMetadata fields)

Define the method-specific identifier grammar: did:bolyra:<commitment> where <commitment> is a 32-byte hex-encoded Poseidon commitment; distinguish human (enrollment commitment) vs agent (credential commitment) by a 1-byte type prefix encoded in the identifier

Document the CRUD operations matrix: Create=enroll() call on IdentityRegistry, Read=resolve() via registry view + DIDDocument construction, Update=N/A for humans (commitment is immutable) / key rotation TBD for agents, Deactivate=revoke() on registry

Add getEnrollmentStatus(bytes32 commitment) and getMerkleRoot() view functions to IdentityRegistry.sol; emit structured Enrolled(commitment, merkleRoot, timestamp) and Revoked(commitment, timestamp) events for off-chain indexing
Recompile and re-test contracts to confirm new view functions return correct state without altering existing storage layout or breaking existing verifier integration tests
Implement did.ts DID parser: validate method prefix, extract commitment bytes, determine subject type (human/agent), return structured ParsedDID object; throw invalidDid on malformed input

Implement resolver.ts DIDResolver: (1) parse DID, (2) query IdentityRegistry.getEnrollmentStatus() — return notFound or deactivated as appropriate, (3) fetch current Merkle root, (4) construct verificationMethod array — BabyJubJub JWK for humans using the commitment as public key fingerprint, EdDSA operator key for agents extracted from on-chain credential metadata, (5) set authentication and assertionMethod relationship arrays, (6) populate DIDDocumentMetadata with created/updated timestamps from events, (7) return DIDResolutionResult

Define the BabyJubJub JWK representation (crv: Baby-Jubjub, kty: OKP) following draft-ietf-cose-bls-key-representations as closest precedent; document the encoding in spec/did-method-bolyra.md §Verification Methods

Write conformance test vectors in spec/conformance/did-resolution-vectors.json covering: valid human DID, valid agent DID, unknown commitment (notFound), revoked commitment (deactivated), syntactically invalid DID (invalidDid)

Write sdk/test/resolver.test.ts using Hardhat in-process node: deploy IdentityRegistry, enroll a test identity, resolve its DID, assert DIDDocument shape matches spec; repeat for agent credential path and revocation path
Update spec/did-method-bolyra.md with the full normative resolution algorithm section, CRUD matrix table, error code table, and DIDDocument example for both human and agent subjects
Write docs/did-resolution-quickstart.md with universal-resolver driver registration steps and SDK usage example
Run full test suite (npm test) and typecheck (cd sdk && npm run typecheck) to confirm no regressions
Commit with DCO sign-off: git commit -s
dependencies: contracts/src/IdentityRegistry.sol — must expose getEnrollmentStatus() and getMerkleRoot() view functions
circuits/build/ — AgentPolicy vkey needed to locate operator public key format for agent verificationMethod
spec/did-method-bolyra.md — existing partial spec to extend rather than replace
W3C DID Core 1.0 §7.1 resolution algorithm and §5 DIDDocument data model
draft-ietf-cose-bls-key-representations or equivalent for BabyJubJub JWK crv name
@bolyra/sdk package.json — DIDResolver interface must be exported from public API surface

winner.title: Standard proof envelope with application/bolyra+json content type
winner.description: Framework integrations all need to serialize/deserialize proof payloads, but each will invent its own format without a standard envelope. Define a JSON schema for BolyraProofEnvelope: {version, circuit, publicSignals[], proof{pi_a, pi_b, pi_c}, metadata{prover, timestamp, chainId}}. Register application/bolyra+json as the content type. All SDK methods (TS and Python) should accept and return this envelope. This prevents every framework adapter from reinventing serialization and makes proofs portable across LangChain↔CrewAI↔AutoGen pipelines.

outline.title: Standard proof envelope with application/bolyra+json content type

artifacts.description: JSON Schema (draft-07) for BolyraProofEnvelope: version, circuit, publicSignals[], proof{pi_a, pi_b, pi_c}, metadata{prover, timestamp, chainId, sessionNonce}
IETF-style spec registering application/bolyra+json, defining canonicalization rules, version negotiation, and forward-compatibility constraints
TypeScript BolyraProofEnvelope type + codec: serializeEnvelope(), deserializeEnvelope(), validateEnvelope() using ajv against the JSON Schema
Update public exports: proveHandshake and verifyHandshake return/accept BolyraProofEnvelope instead of raw snarkjs output objects
Python BolyraProofEnvelope dataclass + from_dict/to_dict; validate() using jsonschema against bundled proof-envelope-schema.json
Ensure envelope.py ships typed stubs — no change needed if marker already present
Replace ad-hoc dict packing with envelope codec; add Content-Type: application/bolyra+json header when posting proofs over HTTP
Round-trip tests: serialize→deserialize identity; schema validation rejects missing fields; version mismatch raises BolyraVersionError
Python parity tests mirroring TS round-trip + validation cases; cross-language fixture: JSON produced by TS deserializable by Python
Developer guide: envelope fields, version lifecycle, how to set Accept/Content-Type headers, migration from raw snarkjs output

steps: Draft spec/proof-envelope-schema.json defining the canonical BolyraProofEnvelope structure with required fields (version, circuit, publicSignals, proof) and optional metadata (prover, timestamp, chainId, sessionNonce)

Write spec/draft-bolyra-proof-envelope-00.md registering application/bolyra+json, specifying JSON canonicalization (RFC 8785), version string format (semver), and forward-compatibility rule (unknown metadata keys MUST be preserved)
Implement sdk/src/envelope.ts with BolyraProofEnvelope TypeScript type, ajv-based validateEnvelope(), serializeEnvelope() (deterministic key order), and deserializeEnvelope() with version gating

Update sdk/src/index.ts: wrap proveHandshake return value in serializeEnvelope(); update verifyHandshake to accept BolyraProofEnvelope and call deserializeEnvelope() internally — maintain backward-compat overload accepting raw object

Bundle proof-envelope-schema.json into sdk-python package (include_package_data); implement sdk-python/bolyra/envelope.py BolyraProofEnvelope dataclass with from_dict/to_dict and validate() using jsonschema
Update sdk-python subprocess bridge: wrap JS prover output in BolyraProofEnvelope.from_dict() before returning to caller
Patch integrations/langchain serialization layer to use envelope codec and emit Content-Type: application/bolyra+json on outbound HTTP proof delivery
Write sdk/test/envelope.test.ts covering round-trip identity, schema rejection on missing proof.pi_a, version mismatch error, and metadata passthrough
Write sdk-python/tests/test_envelope.py with matching cases plus a cross-language fixture test loading a JSON file produced by the TS serializer
Add cross-language fixture file sdk/test/fixtures/envelope_v1.json checked into repo; both TS and Python tests assert they can deserialize it
Run npm run typecheck in sdk/ and pytest -v in sdk-python/; fix any type errors
Write docs/proof-envelope.md covering field definitions, version lifecycle, HTTP Content-Type usage, and migration path from raw snarkjs output
Update sdk/QUICKSTART.md to show envelope-first examples replacing raw object examples
Commit all changes with DCO sign-off (git commit -s)
dependencies: ajv (>=8) — already transitive via snarkjs in sdk/
jsonschema (>=4.0) — add to sdk-python/pyproject.toml if not present
RFC 8785 JSON Canonicalization Scheme — spec reference only, no library needed for initial implementation
@bolyra/sdk v0.2.0 public API (proveHandshake, verifyHandshake) — must stay backward-compatible

winner.title: Cross-chain Merkle root sync via EIP-1186 storage proofs
winner.description: Deploy IdentityRegistry on Arbitrum and Polygon alongside the existing Base Sepolia deployment. Implement a CrossChainRootRelay contract that accepts EIP-1186 storage proofs of the humanTree and agentTree roots from the canonical chain (Base) and validates them against the L1 state root available via each L2's precompile (ArbSys.arbBlockHash on Arbitrum, StateSender on Polygon). This eliminates reliance on trusted relayers — any party can relay a root update by submitting the storage proof. The relay contract maintains the same 30-root history buffer as the primary registry.

outline.title: Cross-chain Merkle root sync via EIP-1186 storage proofs

artifacts.description: Abstract base relay contract: accepts EIP-1186 storage proofs, verifies account + storage proof against an L1 state root, writes humanTree/agentTree roots into a 30-slot ring buffer, emits RootSynced events.
CrossChainRootRelay specialisation for Arbitrum: fetches L1 block hash via ArbSys.arbBlockHash(blockNumber) and passes it as the trusted state root anchor to the base proof verifier.
CrossChainRootRelay specialisation for Polygon PoS: reads the L1 state root from the StateSender / CheckpointManager checkpoint and passes it to the base proof verifier.
Pure library: Patricia-Merkle trie proof verification for EIP-1186 account proof (RLP-encoded) and storage proof. Returns the decoded storage value or reverts on invalid proof.
Extend existing IdentityRegistry to expose humanTreeRoot and agentTreeRoot as public storage slots at deterministic positions (packed, slot 0 and 1) so EIP-1186 proofs reference stable slot keys.
Hardhat Ignition / deploy script: deploys IdentityRegistry + ArbitrumRootRelay on Arbitrum Sepolia; wires relay to registry address on Base Sepolia.
Deploy script for Polygon Amoy testnet: deploys IdentityRegistry + PolygonRootRelay, wires to Base Sepolia canonical registry address.

Hardhat unit tests: mock ArbSys / CheckpointManager precompiles, generate fixture EIP-1186 proofs from a known trie, assert correct root extraction, ring-buffer rotation, replay protection, and revert on invalid proof.
Fuzz + property tests for the Patricia-Merkle trie verifier library: valid proofs pass, bit-flipped proofs revert, wrong slot key reverts, empty-value proofs handled correctly.

TypeScript helpers: fetchStorageProof(provider, registryAddress, blockNumber) wraps eth_getProof; encodeRelayCalldata(proof) ABI-encodes the proof struct for relayRoot(); pollRootSync(relayAddress, chain) waits for RootSynced event.

Protocol spec: canonical chain designation, proof freshness window (max 256 blocks on Arbitrum due to ArbSys limit), ring-buffer semantics, security assumptions (L1 finality, no reorg beyond buffer depth), threat model for proof submission race conditions.
Operator guide: how to fetch an EIP-1186 proof, call relayRoot() on each L2, monitor RootSynced events, and run a permissionless relay bot using the SDK helpers.

steps: Pin humanTreeRoot and agentTreeRoot to deterministic storage slots (slot 0 and slot 1) in IdentityRegistry.sol and document slot keys in spec.
Implement EIP1186Verifier.sol library: RLP decoding, Patricia-Merkle trie node traversal, account proof verification, storage proof verification against account storageHash.

Implement CrossChainRootRelay.sol abstract base: storage-proof intake struct, 30-slot ring buffer for (root, blockNumber, timestamp) tuples, abstract _getL1StateRoot(blockNumber) hook, relayRoot() entry point, replay-protection via processed blockNumber bitmap.
Implement ArbitrumRootRelay.sol: override _getL1StateRoot to call ArbSys(0x64).arbBlockHash(blockNumber); enforce blockNumber within 256-block ArbSys window.
Implement PolygonRootRelay.sol: override _getL1StateRoot to read checkpointRoot from ICheckpointManager at the Polygon-standard address for the given checkpoint index.
Write EIP1186Verifier unit tests with pre-computed fixture tries; achieve 100% branch coverage including empty-value and extension-node edge cases.
Write CrossChainRootRelay unit tests with mock precompile contracts; test ring-buffer wrap-around at index 30, duplicate-block replay rejection, and mismatched storage slot key rejection.
Write Hardhat deploy scripts for Arbitrum Sepolia and Polygon Amoy; parameterise canonical registry address and precompile addresses via environment variables.
Add SDK fetchStorageProof() using eth_getProof JSON-RPC call against an archive node; validate returned proof structure before encoding.
Add SDK encodeRelayCalldata() to ABI-encode the EIP-1186 proof struct matching the Solidity layout.
Write integration test (forked Base Sepolia + Arbitrum Sepolia): enroll a human on Base, fetch storage proof, relay to Arbitrum relay contract, assert ring buffer updated correctly.
Update spec/cross-chain-root-sync.md with finality assumptions, proof freshness window, and ring-buffer depth rationale.
Write docs/cross-chain-relay-guide.md with end-to-end relay bot example using SDK helpers.
Add Hardhat task relay:arb and relay:polygon that automate the fetch-prove-submit cycle for CI smoke tests.
Run npm run test:contracts to confirm all existing and new tests pass; run tsc --noEmit on SDK; sign all commits with DCO.
dependencies: ethers.js >= 6.x (eth_getProof support)
@arbitrum/sdk or direct ArbSys ABI at 0x0000000000000000000000000000000000000064
ICheckpointManager ABI (Polygon PoS, Amoy testnet address)
RLP decoding library compatible with Solidity (inline or via rlp-decode npm for test fixture generation)
Hardhat multi-chain deploy support (hardhat-ignition or custom deploy scripts with --network flag)
Archive node access for Base Sepolia (eth_getProof requires archive state)
Existing IdentityRegistry.sol with storage layout stabilised before slot keys are committed to spec

winner.title: Human tree root history buffer (parity with agent tree)
winner.description: The agentTree has a 30-root history buffer preventing proof staleness during concurrent enrollments, but humanTree has none — any new human enrollment instantly invalidates all in-flight human proofs. Add the same `uint256[30] humanRootHistory` ring buffer to IdentityRegistry.sol and update `verifyHandshake` to check membership in the buffer. Without this, production deployments with >1 human enrolling concurrently will see random handshake failures, which is a dealbreaker for any real adoption.

outline.title: Human tree root history buffer (parity with agent tree)

artifacts.description: Add `uint256[30] public humanRootHistory` ring buffer and `uint256 public humanRootHistoryIndex` cursor. On every `enrollHuman` call, write the new Merkle root into `humanRootHistory[humanRootHistoryIndex % 30]` and increment `humanRootHistoryIndex`. Update `verifyHandshake` to accept a `humanMerkleRoot` that exists anywhere in `humanRootHistory` (linear scan over 30 slots), not just the current `humanMerkleRoot` storage slot.

Expose `humanRootHistory(uint256 index) external view returns (uint256)` and `humanRootHistoryIndex() external view returns (uint256)` in the interface to allow off-chain SDK queries for staleness checks.

Hardhat tests covering: (1) single enrollment writes root to slot 0; (2) 30 sequential enrollments fill all slots; (3) 31st enrollment overwrites slot 0 (ring wrap); (4) a handshake proof generated against root N-1 still verifies after enrollment N; (5) a proof generated against root N-31 (evicted) is rejected; (6) concurrent enrollment simulation — two proofs generated at the same root both verify after a third enrollment lands.

Update `verifyHandshake` helper to query `humanRootHistory` on-chain and surface a `STALE_HUMAN_ROOT` typed error when the proof root is not found in the buffer, giving callers actionable feedback before submitting a doomed transaction.

Add a paragraph under §4 (Registry State) specifying the 30-slot ring buffer semantics for both human and agent trees, the eviction policy (oldest slot overwritten), and the security implication: provers must regenerate proofs after more than 30 concurrent enrollments have landed.

Add a note in the 'Concurrent Enrollments' section warning that proofs are valid only while the root they commit to remains in the 30-slot history buffer, and recommend callers check proof freshness before submitting handshakes in high-throughput deployments.

steps: Read existing `enrollAgent` / `agentRootHistory` implementation in IdentityRegistry.sol to confirm exact ring-buffer pattern to mirror.
Add `uint256[30] public humanRootHistory` and `uint256 public humanRootHistoryIndex` state variables immediately after the analogous agent fields for layout symmetry.

In `enrollHuman(uint256 newRoot)`: replace `humanMerkleRoot = newRoot` with `humanRootHistory[humanRootHistoryIndex % 30] = newRoot; humanRootHistoryIndex++; humanMerkleRoot = newRoot;` (retain the scalar for cheap current-root reads by external tooling).
Extract a private `_humanRootValid(uint256 root) internal view returns (bool)` helper that iterates the 30-slot array and returns true on any match — mirrors the existing `_agentRootValid` pattern.
Replace the single-equality root check in `verifyHandshake` with `require(_humanRootValid(humanMerkleRoot), 'STALE_HUMAN_ROOT')`.
Update `IIdentityRegistry.sol` interface with the two new view functions (`humanRootHistory`, `humanRootHistoryIndex`).
Run `npm run compile:contracts` and confirm zero compiler errors; fix any storage layout or interface mismatch warnings.
Write Hardhat tests in `contracts/test/IdentityRegistry.humanRootHistory.test.ts` covering all six scenarios listed in the test artifact description.
Run `npm run test:contracts` and confirm all existing and new tests pass.
Update `sdk/src/registry.ts` to expose `STALE_HUMAN_ROOT` typed error from the `verifyHandshake` helper.
Update spec and docs artifacts with the 30-slot semantics and concurrency guidance.
Commit with DCO sign-off: `git commit -s -m 'feat(contracts): add humanRootHistory ring buffer for proof staleness tolerance'`.
dependencies: contracts/src/IdentityRegistry.sol — existing agentRootHistory ring buffer implementation to mirror
contracts/src/IIdentityRegistry.sol — interface must stay in sync with new view functions
pot16.ptau — no circuit changes required; HumanUniqueness circuit already outputs humanMerkleRoot as a public signal, so no re-ceremony needed
Hardhat 2.x — test suite for contract-level ring buffer verification
@bolyra/sdk — registry.ts staleness check depends on the updated interface ABI
